title: Aurora Galactica Player Guide
strapline: A quick, player-first guide to starting the game, reading the screen, staying alive, and using the key in-game controls.
currentGoal: Help a new player start quickly, understand the cabinet-style HUD, and make sense of the capture/rescue loop without needing developer context.

sections.title: Start Fast
sections.summary: The shortest path from opening the game to surviving your first run.

cards.title: Start A Run
cards.body: Press Enter to begin from the title/wait screen. The current development build may start from a selected stage if Developer Tools were used, but the normal player flow starts at Stage 1.

cards.title: Basic Controls
cards.body: Move with Arrow Left/Right or A / D. Fire with Space. Pause with P or the pause icon on the right rail. Cabinet-style left-hand play is also supported on the web: Ctrl moves left and Command moves right. Mac browsers do not reliably expose the Fn key.

cards.title: Use The Rail
cards.body: The right-side icon rail keeps key actions in the game: guide, controls, scores, account, bug report, mute, pause, and settings. You should not need to leave the game to learn or report something.

sections.title: Read The Screen
sections.summary: The HUD follows classic Galaga conventions closely enough that the key signals should be familiar once you know where to look.
bullets: Bottom-right stage markers track your current stage in Galaga-style count markers.
Reserve ships at the lower-left show how many lives remain after the current fighter.
Short center-screen banners explain captures, rescues, transitions, and challenge-stage results.

sections.title: How To Survive
sections.summary: The game is still about lane reading and timing, not spraying bullets constantly.

cards.title: Prioritize Descending Enemies
cards.body: The biggest danger usually comes from enemies diving or converging into your lane. Clear close threats early and avoid getting trapped between bullets and a descending enemy.

cards.title: Move Early, Not Late
cards.body: The ship rewards small early corrections more than last-second swerves. If a lane is becoming dangerous, slide out before the dive path closes on you.

cards.title: Know When To Stop Shooting
cards.body: You have an arcade-style shot limit. If the screen is already carrying your shot, focus on positioning instead of hammering fire and expecting another projectile immediately.

sections.title: Capture And Rescue
sections.summary: Bosses can capture your fighter, and later you can rescue it for a stronger dual-fighter attack.
bullets: A boss using the tractor beam can pull your fighter upward if you stay in the beam.
If you break the capture at the right moment, your ship returns and the boss retreats.
If the fighter is fully captured, the boss may carry it later during an attack.
Destroying the carrying boss can release the captured fighter and let it rejoin you.
A successful rescue creates a dual-fighter state with wider firepower.

sections.title: Challenge Stage
sections.summary: Challenge stages are a scoring break: fewer survival demands, more emphasis on accuracy.
bullets: Focus on clean hits and rhythm rather than panic movement.
The challenge stage is the best place to recover mentally before the next live combat stage.
Perfect or high-accuracy clears are valuable, but surviving the normal stages still matters more overall.

sections.title: Helpful In-Game Tools
sections.summary: The game includes a few player-support surfaces beyond the core action.

cards.title: Controls Overlay
cards.body: Use the joystick icon to open a compact controls index without leaving the game.

cards.title: Bug Report
cards.body: Use the bug icon to report gameplay or visual problems directly from the rail.

cards.title: Pilot Account And Scores
cards.body: Use the account and trophy icons to manage your profile and review score tables.
